originalTitle: Bay Area city manager charged for allegedly breaking into 66ft yacht while drinking
summary: Sausalito city manager Elaine Forbes faces misdemeanor charges for allegedly trying to steal the three-story vessel
content: A Bay Area city manager will face misdemeanor charges for allegedly breaking into a 66ft yacht while drinking and trying to steal the vessel. Elaine Forbes, Sausalito’s city manager, was arrested on the morning of 18 July after police received a call for a noise disturbance. Police say Forbes had “apparently broken into” a three-story yacht, where she was consuming alcohol. Forbes, who previously served as director of the Port of San Francisco, appeared to make a ruckus on the yacht. The boat’s owner, Andy Cunningham, told ABC7 that she found a bottle of maraschino cherries dumped into a shoebox and four empty bottles of wine. “She had the horn going, the windshield wipers going, the spotlight going,” Capt Bill Aubrey, who called 911 on Forbes, told ABC7. Forbes had been living on a houseboat near the marina where she was arrested, ABC7 reported. Police initially charged Forbes with felony burglary, and her bail was set at $50,000. But prosecutors have since lessened those to misdemeanor charges for loitering, squatting and petty theft, according to Sgt Adam Schermerhorn, a spokesperson for the Marin county sheriff’s department. Yacht theft is not common in Marin county, he said. The Sausalito city council had placed Forbes on leave last week during a closed meeting, according to NBC News. The city council is evaluating her job performance at a meeting today. She was appointed to her position three weeks ago. “As the facts become clearer and Elaine obtains legal and healthcare counsel in the coming hours and days, we hope people will respond with compassion, patience and respect for her and her family during an extraordinarily difficult time,” spokesperson Sam Singer said in a widely circulated statement issued on behalf of the Forbes family.
chineseTitle: 湾区城市经理因涉嫌酒后闯入 66 英尺游艇而被指控
chineseSummary: 索萨利托市经理伊莱恩·福布斯因涉嫌试图盗窃这艘三层船只而面临轻罪指控
chineseContent: 一名湾区城市经理因涉嫌酒后闯入一艘 66 英尺游艇并试图偷窃船只而面临轻罪指控。 7 月 18 日上午，索萨利托市城市经理伊莱恩·福布斯 (Elaine Forbes) 在接到噪音骚扰报警后被捕。警方表示，福布斯“显然闯入”了一艘三层游艇，当时她正在里面喝酒。曾担任旧金山港董事的福布斯似乎在游艇上大吵大闹。船主安迪·坎宁安 (Andy Cunningham) 告诉 ABC7，她发现一瓶樱桃被倒在鞋盒里，还有四瓶空酒。 “她按响了喇叭，打开了挡风玻璃雨刷器，打开了聚光灯，”在《福布斯》上拨打 911 的比尔·奥布里上尉告诉 ABC7。据美国广播公司七台报道，福布斯一直住在她被捕的码头附近的一艘船屋上。警方最初指控福布斯犯有盗窃罪，保释金为 5 万美元。但据马林县治安部门发言人亚当·舍默霍恩中士称，检察官已将这些指控减轻为游荡、擅自占地和小偷小摸等轻罪指控。他说，游艇盗窃在马林县并不常见。据 NBC 新闻报道，索萨利托市议会上周在一次闭门会议上让福布​​斯休假。市议会正在今天的会议上评估她的工作表现。三周前她被任命担任这一职务。发言人萨姆·辛格（Sam Singer）在代表福布斯家族发表的一份广为流传的声明中表示：“随着事实变得更加清晰，伊莱恩将在未来几小时和几天内获得法律和医疗保健咨询，我们希望人们在这个异常困难的时期以同情、耐心和尊重的态度回应她和她的家人。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
